# Batch claim

> **Feature delivered — acceptance criteria.** Generated from the as-built BDD tests in
> [`test/stories/batch_claim_story_test.exs`](../../../echo/apps/echo_mq/test/stories/batch_claim_story_test.exs). Every scenario below is a passing
> ExUnit test driving the real `EchoMQ` surface against Valkey, so the catalogue cannot
> drift from the code.

**3 scenarios.**

## Scenario: a worker claims up to size jobs in one atomic, server-clocked pull

- **Given** a pending set flooded with 10 distinct mint-ordered JOB-ids
- **When** claim_batch is called once for size 4
- **Then** it serves exactly the 4 oldest-mint members in mint order, each at attempts 1
- **And** every served member is leased on ONE shared server-clock deadline (one TIME read)

## Scenario: an under-fill is a short batch, and a paused queue answers empty untouched

- **Given** a pending set of 2 members and a claim requesting size 5
- **When** claim_batch is called for size 5 with only 2 pending
- **Then** it answers the 2 available members (a short batch, never over-popping, never blocking)
- **And** a subsequent claim on the now-empty pending set answers :empty
- **And** a claim against a queue paused queue-wide answers :empty with the pending set untouched

## Scenario: one poisoned member is isolated to its own retry while the rest settle

- **Given** a batch of 3 members claimed in one call
- **When** the poison member is retried (scheduled) and the other two complete
- **Then** the poison row is scheduled with last_error kept; the other two rows are retired
- **And** after promote a fresh batch finds ONLY the poison, now at attempts 2 (its own token)
